heading: Definitions
label: 7001 Definitions
text: In this title, the term “Army” means the Army or Armies referred to in the Constitution of the United States, less that part established by law as the Air Force.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)30015:181–3(a).10:1a(a)., .June 28, 1950, ch. 383, § 2(a)64 Stat. 263The words “Army of the United States” and “are synonymous and” are omitted as surplusage, since the term “Army” is used throughout the revised title. 5:181–3(a) (last sentence) and 10:1a(a) (last sentence) are omitted as surplusage.
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this title1987— inserted “the term” after “In this title,”.Pub. L. 100–26

A of , see Tables for classification] shall be deemed to refer to the section or chapter as so redesignated.”Pub. L. 115–232
